title: [Panels][Tabs]: work area doesn't switch to already activated connection's tab if it's on the other panel
author: PetbkA
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
Activating the already opened connection from the panel that's not active should activate this panel and connection's tab. I.e. if the active connection is _XP_ on _Windows_ panel, and I'm opening  _Ubuntu_ on _Unix_ panel that has already been opened, work ar...

iterations.description: The application fails to switch to an already activated connection's tab when that connection is located on a different panel, leading to inconsistent UI state and usability issues.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The application fails to switch to an already activated connection's tab when that connection is located on a different panel, leading to inconsistent UI state and usability issues.
Approach: Modify 'SwitchToOpenConnection' to globally search for the open connection tab across all panels, activating the found panel and then its respective tab if the connection is already active.
